comparison.base_feed: gtfs_subway (MTA NYC Transit — regular subway feed)
comparison.important_caveat: These are MTA's two parallel subway products, not sequential versions. The 'supplemented' feed is the base feed enriched with far more granular service patterns (planned service changes, work-train diversions, etc.). Stops, routes, and shapes are essentially identical; the entire difference is in the schedule (trips/stop_times) and how services are modeled (calendar). The new feed is fully self-consistent — every FK resolves.

breaking_changes.detail: Of 20,309 base trip_ids, only 16,924 (83%) survive into the supplemented feed; ~3,385 base trip_ids are absent. Simultaneously 63,807 new trip_ids are added (20,309 → 80,731 total).
breaking_changes.note: The supplemented feed re-derives trip_ids around the new service_id scheme, so 'removed' trips are mostly re-expressed under new IDs rather than dropped service. Still flagged: any base trip_id held externally may not resolve.

suspicious_changes.detail: trip count grew 20,309 → 80,731 (+297.5%); stop_times grew 563,533 → 2,375,743 rows (+321.6%). Both are ~4x, far beyond the 20% threshold.
suspicious_changes.note: Coherent and proportional — the ~4x trips drive the ~4x stop_times, which is exactly what a service-pattern supplement looks like, not a corruption. But it's the largest count delta of any feed pair analyzed and must be surfaced.
Full calendar model swap, tied to the service_id rename above. exception_type domain unchanged {1,2}, so the mechanism is the same, just far more granular.

suspicious_changes.detail: The new feed's feed_info.txt dropped feed_contact_url (present in the base feed at column position 7). With no feed_contact_email supplied either, the new feed triggers a validation WARNING for missing feed contact information. The base feed had no such warning.
suspicious_changes.note: feed_contact_url is optional per the GTFS spec, but its removal alongside absent feed_contact_email is a regression in feed metadata quality. Downstream tooling or portals that surface a contact link for this feed will lose that information.

checks_passed.removed_required_field: none — feed_info.txt dropped feed_contact_url (optional); routes.txt only reordered columns
checks_passed.changed_field_type_or_format: none; all 2,375,743 new stop_times rows valid, including MTA after-midnight times up to hour 28 (28:xx:xx) correctly parsed
checks_passed.referential_integrity_new_feed: clean — stop_times→stops 0 missing, stop_times→trips 0 missing, trips→routes/service/shapes 0 missing, stops→parent_station 0 missing, transfers→stops 0 missing
checks_passed.stops: identical — 1,488 in both, 0 removed, 0 added, 0 moved >500m, bbox unchanged [40.51276,-74.25196]–[40.90313,-73.7554]
checks_passed.routes: identical — 29 in both, 0 removed/added, 0 renamed, 0 type changes
checks_passed.feed_publisher_change: no (MTA New York City Transit in both)
checks_passed.shapes: 246 of 257 shapes common (255 new); trips resolve fully — a near-identical shape set
